返回本批次

美国环保署负责甲烷监管的官员是一名石油和天然气游说者

EPA official in charge of methane regulations was an oil and gas lobbyist

www.propublica.org 作者 Jimmc414 分数 57 评论 8 2026/04/05 22:56
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 领导放宽甲烷污染规定的特朗普政府官员是一位未透露姓名的作者，他在四年前还是一名石油和天然气游说者时，曾发表过反对这些规定的重要行业论据。
  2. Aaron Szabo 是美国环境保护局的助理局长，在 PDF 元数据中被列为 2022 年 1 月一封反对石油和天然气行业甲烷排放控制提议的评论信的作者。这封信是由美国勘探与生产委员会提交给美国环保署的，该委员会代表了该行业一些最大的温室气体排放国，包括康菲石油公司、多元化能源公司和希尔科普公司。 Szabo 的名字没有出现在文档本身中，但可以在用于创建 PDF 文件的软件嵌入的信息中找到。
  3. 萨博在起草反对这些限制的论据时，被登记为 AXPC 鲜为人知的成员之一奥文蒂夫 (Ovintiv) 的说客，这些论据后来在拜登政府中最终确定。他还为石油和化工行业的其他客户进行游说。虽然他在去年确认担任美国环保署空气与辐射办公室主任时没有隐瞒这项工作，但他在描述这项工作时避免提及影响气候政策的努力：“我了解到受监管的实体如何遵守联邦政府的数千项法规和政策。我还亲眼看到在这些公司工作的人们希望确保环境得到适当的保护。”
  4. 根据 ProPublica 审查的内部电子邮件、日历条目和闭门对话记录，萨博目前在美国环保局负责监督联邦气候规则，他一直在征求石油工业团体的意见，甚至是具体的监管语言，这些团体将从淡化的甲烷规则中获益。
  5. 参议院环境与公共工程委员会的资深民主党参议员谢尔登·怀特豪斯 (Sheldon Whitehouse) 指出，萨博之前的游说证明美国环保署实际上已被石油和天然气行业俘获。怀特豪斯在一封电子邮件中告诉ProPublica：“现在他可以在美国环保署内部从事石油巨头的肮脏工作。”

Original Summary

  1. The Trump administration official leading an effort to loosen rules on methane pollution was an unnamed author of key industry arguments against those same rules just four years ago when he was an oil and gas lobbyist.
  2. Aaron Szabo, an assistant administrator at the Environmental Protection Agency, is listed in PDF metadata as the author of a January 2022 comment letter objecting to proposed controls on methane emissions in the oil and gas industry. The letter was submitted to the EPA by the American Exploration and Production Council, which represents some of the industry’s largest emitters of the planet-warming gas, including ConocoPhillips, Diversified Energy and Hilcorp. Szabo’s name does not appear in the document itself, but it can be found in information embedded by the software used to create the PDF file.
  3. Szabo was registered as a lobbyist for one of the AXPC’s lesser-known members, Ovintiv, when he drafted the arguments against the restrictions, which were finalized later in the Biden administration. He has also lobbied for other clients in the oil and chemicals sectors. While he did not hide that work during his confirmation last year as head of the EPA’s Office of Air and Radiation, he described it in terms that avoided any mention of efforts to influence climate policy: “I learned how regulated entities comply with the federal government’s thousands of regulations and policies. I also saw firsthand that the people working in these companies want to ensure the environment is properly protected.”
  4. In his current role overseeing federal climate rules at the EPA, Szabo has been soliciting input and even specific regulatory language from oil industry groups that stand to gain from watered-down methane rules, according to internal emails, calendar entries and records of closed-door conversations reviewed by ProPublica.
  5. Sen. Sheldon Whitehouse, D-R.I., the ranking Democrat on the Senate’s Environment and Public Works Committee, pointed to Szabo’s previous lobbying as evidence that the EPA had effectively been captured by the oil and gas industry. “Now he can do Big Oil’s dirty work from inside the EPA,” Whitehouse told ProPublica in an email.

评论（默认中文）

burkaman 2026/04/05 23:27
重要的一点是，他是受雇写反对他们的论据的。从理论上讲，如果有一个竞争对手团队，其中法规的知识反对者与该机构协商并抵制过度或无效的规则，那会很棒，但这家伙没有原则性的哲学反对意见，他只是为付给他最多钱的人写作。我们可以非常肯定，联邦政府并不是他服务的最高出价者，那么他为什么要在美国环保局工作呢？
The important point is that he was paid to write the argument against them. In theory it would be great to have a sort of team of rivals where intellectual opponents of regulations consult with the agency and push back on excessive or ineffective rules, but this guy doesn't have a principled philosophical objection, he just writes for whoever pays him the most money. We can be pretty certain that the federal government is not the highest bidder for his services, so why is he working at the EPA?

beej71 2026/04/05 23:49
哥们估计钱够多了。现在他追求的是影响力和地位。
Dude probably has enough money. Now he's after influence and status.
在 HN 中查看
leeoniya 2026/04/05 23:24
> 参议员谢尔顿·怀特豪斯

好吧，我有一分钟很困惑。

> 而且，考虑到气体分解得相对较快，这将是减少全球变暖最快的方法之一。

s/快/慢？

> Sen. Sheldon Whitehouse

ok, i was confused for a minute.

> And, given that the gas breaks down relatively quickly, this would have been one of the fastest ways to reduce global warming.

s/quickly/slowly?

在 HN 中查看
brendoelfrendo 2026/04/06 00:33
不，快点是正确的。如果我没记错的话，大气中甲烷的寿命约为 12 年。这意味着，如果停止排放，那么大气中甲烷的变暖效应将在十年内消退。与二氧化碳相比，二氧化碳是一种较弱的温室气体，但不会随着时间的推移自然分解。大气中的二氧化碳必须通过自然过程（海洋吸收、植物光合作用等）吸收，否则它会永远存在于大气中。
No, quickly is correct. Methane in the atmosphere has a lifespan of about 12 years, if I recall correctly. That means that if you stop emitting it, then the warming effect of atmospheric methane would subside over the course of a decade. This is as compared to CO2, which is a much weaker greenhouse gas, but does not break down naturally over time. Atmospheric CO2 has to be absorbed by natural processes (oceanic absorption, plant photosynthesis, etc) otherwise it exists in the atmosphere effectively forever.
在 HN 中查看
lotharcable 2026/04/05 23:47
游说者变成政府监管者的情况比人们意识到的要普遍得多。 无论哪个政党入主白宫，这都是正常的。

这是大多数人没有意识到这种情况正在发生的事情之一，因为它很少被报道。此外，谁参与管理行政机构也是一个无关紧要且非常无聊的话题。这不像你可以投票给他们中的任何一个。

Loabyist 作为首席监管者的情况反映了行政国家的实际运作方式以及大型公共公司在该行政管理中所扮演的角色。

“ELI5”的解释是......

现代政治家都是职业政治家。这意味着他们中的大多数人都是从获得法律学位开始的，然后立即利用他们拥有的任何关系从事政治生涯。 这意味着他们没有法律和政党和政府内部官僚机构之外的世界的真实经验。

这意味着他们在监管任何行业方面都极不合格。他们只是对“现实世界”一无所知。他们对如何制造汽车、冶炼钢铁、钻探石油或设计电灯开关一无所知。

联邦政府400多个行政机构的职业管理者也面临着类似的情况。 他们获得公共管理人员或与其监管职能相关领域的学位。许多高级管理人员都拥有硕士和博士学位。 但对于任何在“私营部门”有经验的人来说，在大学环境中学到的知识与实际应用中的知识之间存在着巨大的差距。

因此，为了填补这一空白，它们需要这些行业的大公司的参与。 他们帮助起草立法，在监管委员会中拥有代表，提供经济分析所需的信息等等。

在美国现代行政国家80年的历史之后，这甚至涉及让企业代表参与政党政治，有时甚至成为政治家，并在监管他们的机构中​​担任职务。

对此的术语是“监管捕获”。

拥有高度监管的行业是有代价的。主要的一点是，通过接受政府的控制，政府承担起确保这些公司保持盈利的责任。

监管费用非常高，对新企业形成了巨大的进入壁垒。 因此，老牌公司可能是某些主要行业中唯一存在的公司。 一旦你失去了它们，它们就永远消失了。

因此，监管越多，政府就越需要对这些企业及其利润提供更多保护。

Lobbyists turning into government regulator is a lot more common then people realize. It is normal regardless of what party occupies the Whitehouse.

This is one of those things were most people don't realize this goes on because it is rarely reported on. Also who is involved in running administrative agencies is largely irrelevant and very boring subject. It isn't like you get to vote for any of them.

Loabyist-as-head-regulator situation is a reflection of how the administrative state actually works and the role that large public corporations play in that administration.

The 'ELI5' explanation is...

Modern politicians are career politicians. Meaning most of them they start off by getting their law degrees and then immediately pursue a political career using whatever connections they have. This means that they have no real experience of the world outside of law and internal bureaucracy of their parties and governments.

This means they are extremely unqualified at regulating any sort of industry. They just don't know anything about 'the real world'. They know nothing about how to make cars, smelt steel, drill for oil or designing light switches.

It is a similar situation for career administrators in the 400+ administrative agencies in the Federal Government. They get degrees in public administrator or in fields related to their regulatory function. Many of the top administrators will have masters and PHDs. But as anybody with experience in the "private sector" there is a very significant gap between what you can learn in a University setting versus what gets applied in actual practice.

So to fill the gap they require the participation of the major corporations in these industries. They help draft legislation, they have representatives in regulatory committees, the provide the information necessary for economic analysis, and so on and so forth.

This, after 80 years of USA history of the modern administrative state, even involves having corporate representatives participate in party politics, sometimes even becoming politicians, and having positions in the agencies that regulate them.

The term for this is "regulatory capture".

There is a trade off for having highly regulated industries. The main one is that by accepting control of the government the government takes on the responsibility of making sure these corporations remain profitable.

The cost of regulatory overhead is very significant and forms a massive barrier of entry for new businesses. So the established corporations are likely the only corporations that are going to exist for some major industries. Once you lose them they are gone forever.

So the more regulation you have the more protective the government needs to be of these businesses and their profits.

在 HN 中查看
Simulacra 2026/04/05 23:51
我对此并不感到惊讶，政府监管中的人还做什么呢？在政府、行业或游说团体工作。无论政党/意识形态如何，这都是一扇旋转门，但我明白。这是一个狭窄的领域。过去政府的监管者现在是游说者，反之亦然。
I am not surprised by this, what else does someone in government regulations do? Work in government, the industry, or lobby. It's all a revolving door regardless of political party/ideology, but I get it. It's a narrow field. Regulators under past Administrations are now lobbyists and vice versa.

jauntywundrkind 2026/04/05 23:34
又是拥有图书馆、地球和整个人类的一天。对人类理性判断、科学和世界的攻击仍在继续！

在社交媒体上，我一直使用主题标签“hostis humanigeneris”。通常在海上环境中用于海盗、奴隶贩子和战犯以及全人类的敌人的拉丁名称。它们让我想起了《文化》系列中的《侮辱》。我怀疑像冒犯这样的政府会很乐意使用这个名字。 https://theculture.fandom.com/wiki/Affront

Just another day owning the libs, and the planet, and humanity in general. The assault against humanity reason judgement science and the world goes on!

On social media, I've been using the hashtag hostis humani generis. A latin designation usually used in maritime contexts for pirates slave traders and war criminals, for enemies of all mankind. They remind me of The Affront in The Culture series. I suspect the administration like that Affront would gladly take the name. https://theculture.fandom.com/wiki/Affront

Billionaires and their lobbyists and politicians need to be declared outlaws in the historical sense for the survival of the species.
